title: Monday Contact Improv: Class + Jam
description: Marie Chabert teaches a thematic CI class with somatic guided exploration, partner work and technique, followed by a 45-60 minute open jam. Explicitly welcomes beginners through to seasoned professionals; doors 6:20pm.

title: Thursday: Singles Pottery Night
description: Singles pottery-painting night by the Thursday dating app — dating plus a hands-on craft in one evening.

title: Thought Experiments in Pubs: Living with Tech
description: Small-group discussion evening exploring technology and AI through thought experiments and rotating conversations.

title: Big Dork Summer: Coach Party at Sebright Arms
description: Isle of Wight indie-rockers Coach Party play an up-close basement show as part of the Big Dork Summer series at this Bethnal Green pub venue.

title: Regent's Park Outdoor CI Jam
description: Free outdoor summer contact improvisation jam with rotating facilitators: social time from 6pm, opening circle 6:40pm, jamming until 8:45pm. Runs Wednesday evenings weekly through 19 August; check the listing for the exact meeting spot.

title: Cowgirls & Egyptians: Blue Blow and Witchy Brews (Darren Le Baron)
description: Evening talk tracing Goddess wisdom, entheogenic sacraments and Egyptian mystery traditions through African cosmology, from the teacher behind the Shroomshop workshops.

title: Thursday: 30+ Singles Night — new venue, Shoreditch
description: 30+ singles night by the Thursday dating app at a just-opened Shoreditch venue.

title: Shroomshop: Mushroom Cultivation Workshop
description: Hands-on evening workshop covering grain spawn, fruiting tubs and aftercare for growing gourmet mushrooms at home; everyone leaves with their own grow kit.

title: Thursday: Singles Night — new venue, Shoreditch
description: Singles night by the Thursday dating app at a just-opened Shoreditch venue.

title: Dancing Freedom in the Woods
venue: Woodland, Hackney Wick (exact spot shared on booking)
description: Substance-free ecstatic dance in a Hackney Wick woodland with a DJ-led wave through expansive musical genres, closing with a fireside gathering for integration and connection.

title: HYPER JAPAN Festival 2026
description: The UK's largest Japanese culture festival runs 24–26 July with over 530 exhibitors covering music, food, fashion, gaming, workshops and shopping. Friday hours 12–8pm; children 10 and under free.

title: MATS X LISA X LASR SUMMER SOCIAL
venue: London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA), 25 Holywell Row, London EC2A 4XE, UK
description:
Get up-to-date information at: https://luma.com/ecrfbfey

Hosted by LISA

title: True Dating: Speed Dating in Bank (ages 30-45)
description: Friday-night mixed speed dating for ages 30-45 at The Wren Tavern by St Paul's; male tickets nearly gone at time of checking.

title: Campfire Club: Junior Brother + Anna McLuckie
description: Friday-evening outdoor Campfire Club session at Cody Dock with Irish alt-folk songwriter Junior Brother and harpist-singer Anna McLuckie. Price not yet listed (the 10 July edition is £20).

title: Discourse: Discoveries at the Icy Moons of Jupiter and Saturn
description: A free evening Discourse lecture at the Royal Institution on the latest discoveries at the icy moons of Jupiter and Saturn, in the famous Faraday lecture theatre.

title: CGI Goth Wife: EP Release Show at The Victoria Dalston
description: EP release party in the back room of this Victorian pub venue on Kingsland Road. Doors 7:30pm, with a free indie club night following.

title: Terrarium Making Workshop with London Terrariums
description: A 90-minute hands-on workshop building your own glass terrarium to take home, run by specialists London Terrariums. Further dates through late July and August, including evening sessions on 29 July and 13 August.

title: PsySoc Community Walk, Speed-Friending & Nature Connection
venue: Hampstead Heath (meeting point emailed after booking)
description: Psychedelic Society guided heath walk mixing mindfulness, meditation and speed-friending — open to all, solo attendance encouraged.

title: Campaign Lab Summer BBQ 2026
description: Casual all-day summer gathering for the Campaign Lab community with food, drinks and networking.

title: A Bohm Dialogue on Love
description: Group inquiry using David Bohm's dialogue method to explore love and connection through deep listening in a non-hierarchical circle — close kin to circling and authentic relating practice.

title: CATS at Regent's Park Open Air Theatre (opening night)
description: A brand-new open-air production of Andrew Lloyd Webber's musical based on T.S. Eliot's Old Possum's Book of Practical Cats, running 25 July – 19 September. Evening performance time approximate.

title: Ceroc SouthKen2 — Two Room Freestyle
description: Friendly two-room Ceroc freestyle: main hall with upbeat Ceroc plus a SILC/chillout room; air-con, licensed bar. Doors 19:45.

title: Summer Sundays at Victoria Park Bandstand: Hackney Community Orchestra + Disco Lizards
description: Free Sunday bandstand session with the Hackney Community Orchestra and Disco Lizards — picnic-blanket format in the park.

title: Garden Gigs: Sidney Jones / Cry 99 / Pigeon Dog
description: Free Sunday-afternoon outdoor session in The Victoria's pub garden — part of a summer-long Garden Gigs series (2-5pm every Sunday) with three up-and-coming live acts.

title: Resistance Ecstatic Dance & Yoga
description: Sunday evening freeform ecstatic dance journey at a Greenwich garden plot with opening circle, guided warmup and a wind-down around the bonfire with tea and snacks.

title: MOTION LONDON — 5Rhythms Movement Gathering
venue: Goldsmiths Great Hall, Richard Hoggart Building
description: Large-scale movement gathering dancing through the 5Rhythms map with live DJ on a big wooden dance floor — 'nothing to learn and everything to remember'. No phones, no spectating. Also runs Mon 24 Aug.

title: Mini Golf Singles Party (ages 28-42) — True Dating
description: Speed-dating alternative where singles aged 28-42 mingle over rounds of tech-enhanced mini golf at Puttshack in the City. Tickets still available for both men and women at time of checking.

title: Emotional Freedom Inquiry — somatic release circle
description: Gentle somatic inquiry group exploring emotional release through body awareness and presence (Sedona Method and non-duality influenced), with individual work witnessed in a compassionate circle. No experience needed.

title: PsySoc Social: Speed-Friending & Psychedelic Storytelling
description: Structured 10-minute speed-friending conversations with connection-deepening questions, followed by a 5-minute open-mic of psychedelic experience stories, at vegan restaurant/bar Love Shack.

title: Simply Being 5Rhythms Full Moon Garden Session
description: Outdoor full-moon 5Rhythms garden session with Richard Wiltshire in Brockwell Park.

title: atproto.london summer drinks
description: Casual summer drinks on Newspeak House's covered terrace for the Bluesky/atproto decentralised-social community — users and developers welcome, no technical focus required.

title: Lau Ro at Servant Jazz Quarters
description: São Paulo-born, Brighton-based artist Lau Ro plays an intimate live set of Brazilian-rooted psych-folk at the 100-capacity Servant Jazz Quarters.

title: Heart Circle — pause, reconnect & explore
description: A slow, held sharing circle using meditation, somatic practice and shared inquiry to deepen connection with self, community and nature — the closest thing to authentic relating in London this window.

title: Sunset Sessions — open-air ecstatic dance (31 July edition)
venue: Outdoor location near Baker Street / Regent's Park (exact spot sent after booking)
description: Sober open-air ecstatic dance on grass with silent-disco headphones playing Afro-House and world beats, plus cacao ceremony and optional pre-dance picnic social from 16:30.

title: West London Supper Club – Summer Edition
description: An intimate 12-seat supper club with a curated multi-course Persian menu and welcome drink, designed for conversation and meeting new people in a cosy Kensington cafe-gallery.

title: Folk Critique — book launch + live sets
description: Double book launch on folk revivalism and resistance with live sets from Angeline Morrison, the Black British Folk Collective, Mataio Austin-Dean and banjo player Jacken Elswyth, plus readings.

title: Big Scary Indian (NYC) + Mr Speed + Teleboutique at The Shacklewell Arms
description: New York's Big Scary Indian heads a cheap three-band Friday bill in the Shacklewell's back room. Doors 8pm.

title: Thursday: Sauna Rave at Sanctuary &Soul
description: Singles edition of the Shoreditch sauna rave, run with the Thursday dating app at Sanctuary &Soul.

title: Worm Time Dialogues — Lammas (Bohm dialogue)
description: Facilitated Bohm dialogue circle exploring our relationship with time and natural rhythms, paired with 'worm time' creative rest. Same practice lineage as the Bohm Dialogue on Love.

title: Ceroc London 50:50 — 6-hour Saturday Freestyle
description: Six-hour Saturday-afternoon Ceroc freestyle near St Pancras: up-tempo Ceroc 2-5pm, then chilled SILC-friendly grooves 5-8pm; tea and snacks included.

title: Crash Of Rhinos at MOTH Club
address: Old Trades Hall, Valette Street, Hackney, E9 6NU
description: Cult Derby emo/post-hardcore band Crash Of Rhinos play a reunion show at Hackney's MOTH Club. Doors 7pm.

title: Intuitive Painting Workshop @ Plot 9
description: Creative Sunday morning of guided meditation and process-led intuitive painting at the Plot 9 garden — no outcome required, all materials provided. (Clashes with the Connection circling day if that runs 2 Aug.)

title: Connection: Our Deepest Desire and Greatest Fear — Circling Day Workshop
description: Full-day facilitated Circling and authentic relating practice with Robyn Wilford — this appears to be the 'Authentic Relating Day London' under its event name. DATE AMBIGUITY: the Eventbrite date says Sun 2 Aug but the description references Sun 9 Aug; confirm with the organiser before booking (robyncontact365@gmail.com).

title: Song Circle @ Plot 9
description: Monthly outdoor campfire-style singing circle taught by call-and-response — folk songs, harmonies and nature-themed pieces, all voices and abilities welcome.

title: Thursday: 30+ Pop Culture Quiz Night
description: Singles-only pub quiz for the 30+ crowd at 21Soho — teams formed on the night, so you meet people while competing.

title: 10 Cups Supper Club
description: 'Haute cuisine in Hackney' — an intimate four-hour supper club evening on Dalston Lane. Price not shown; check the Eventbrite page.

title: Land and Belonging: Josh Schrei and Alexander Beiner in Conversation
description: The Emerald podcast's Josh Schrei and sensemaking writer Alexander Beiner explore immigration, land and belonging through a mythic lens, hosted by the Psychedelic Society.

title: Thursday: Singles Darts Night at Bat & Ball
description: Mixed singles social darts night — activity-led icebreaker format rather than a standing-around bar night.

title: CLR Summer Fellowship Speaker Series: Chi Nguyen (Redwood Research)
venue: London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA), 25 Holywell Row, London EC2A 4XE, UK
description:
Get up-to-date information at: https://luma.com/67nhmqfy

title: Speed Dating + Speed Karaoke at MOTH Club
address: Old Trades Hall, Valette Street, E9 6NU
description: Mixed speed dating for people in their 20s and 30s, followed by speed karaoke under MOTH Club's glitter ceiling. Doors 7pm; come solo or bring a single friend.

title: Contact Beyond Contact (CBC) Workshop
description: Movement workshop blending Contact Improvisation with Authentic Movement, Qi Gong and somatic practice, run by Cocoon; no dance experience required. Strong crossover of contact improv and authentic relating.

title: Slow Mass at The Victoria Dalston
description: Chicago post-hardcore/indie outfit Slow Mass bring their intricate, heavy melodies to the Victoria's small back-room stage. Doors 7pm.

title: Robert Vincent at Green Note
description: Award-winning Liverpool Americana singer-songwriter plays an intimate show at Camden's dedicated folk/roots cafe venue. Doors 7pm.

title: Thursday: Boxpark Shoreditch Singles Night
description: Thursday's flagship mixed singles bar night takes over Boxpark Shoreditch, five minutes' ride away, with icebreakers and hosts on hand.

title: Ebony — live at Village Underground
description: Evening gig by Ebony at the converted Victorian warehouse venue in Shoreditch, a short walk from Bethnal Green.

title: The Next Chapter of Psychedelics with Robin Carhart-Harris
description: Neuroscientist Robin Carhart-Harris discusses where psychedelic science goes next, including his REBUS model and forthcoming book, followed by social mingling.

title: Jeffrey Alexander & The Heavy Lidders + Christina Carter
description: Psychedelic improvisational folk-rock from Jeffrey Alexander (Dire Wolves), joined by Christina Carter of Charalambides on wordless vocals for this UK tour date.

title: The BIG SILC Freestyle (Ceroc/SILC/WCS)
description: Special chill-out freestyle in the big main hall at South Kensington: four hours of chilled and funky grooves for Ceroc, SILC and WCS dancers, 8pm to midnight.

title: Thursday: 30+ Cocktails & Singles at Fonda
description: Sunday-afternoon mixed singles session for 30+ at Mexican restaurant-bar Fonda — cocktails and a daytime, conversation-first format.

title: Simply Being 5Rhythms New Moon Eclipse Garden Special
description: Outdoor new-moon eclipse 5Rhythms garden special with Richard Wiltshire in Brockwell Park.

title: Jeffrey Silverstein + Bobby Lee at MOTH Club
address: Old Trades Hall, Valette Street, E9 6NU
description: Portland cosmic-country/ambient-folk songwriter Jeffrey Silverstein headlines with UK pastoral-twang guitarist Bobby Lee in support.

title: Shoreditch Speed Dating @ Be At One (ages 23-35)
description: Mixed speed dating from True Dating for ages 23-35: up to 15 five-minute mini-dates at Be At One on Old Street.

title: Uyen Luu's Vietnamese Supper Club (8 courses)
venue: Uyen Luu's studio (address emailed after booking)
description: Long-running Hackney supper club by cookbook author Uyen Luu: eight courses of homemade Vietnamese food at one long shared table near Broadway Market. Also runs 24/25/31 Jul and most Fri/Sat in August.

title: Dunajska Kapelye — Balkan folk & klezmer at Green Note
description: Green Note favourites tear through Balkan and Gypsy folk, hot-club jazz, klezmer and tango — rip-roaring East European party folk. Doors 7pm.

title: Bengal to Bethnal Green — Grand Union Orchestra
description: Backyard Biennial evening celebrating Bangladeshi heritage: homemade food from women-led Oitij-jo Kitchen from 6pm, then a performance blending Bengali ragas with Grand Union Orchestra's global musicians, plus Q&A.

title: closeup summer series: Eaves Wilder at Sebright Arms
description: Rising London indie-pop songwriter Eaves Wilder plays a stripped-back basement show as part of closeup's 2026 summer series.

title: London Ceilidh Club at Cecil Sharp House
description: Saturday-night Scottish ceilidh with a live band and a caller walking everyone through the dances — no partner or experience needed, a natural fit for a Ceroc dancer. Also runs Fridays 24/31 July and 7 August.

title: Alexander Technique: An Introduction (City Academy)
description: A 3-hour evening introduction to the Alexander Technique with teacher Cristina Codina Llavina, focused on improving posture and releasing habitual tension. Open to complete beginners.

title: Words From the Other Side: Poetry as a Practice for Altered States
description: Hands-on two-hour writing workshop with Ryan Khan using grounding practices, sensory exploration and playful low-pressure prompts to process altered-states experiences through poetry; non-alcoholic drinks included.

title: Greg Freeman at MOTH Club
address: Old Trades Hall, Valette Street, E9 6NU
description: Vermont indie/alt-country songwriter Greg Freeman; first of two nights (also 19 Aug if this sells out). NOTE: Songkick lists this as Wed 19 Aug while DICE says 18 Aug — check the ticket page before travelling.

title: Devon Again at MOTH Club
address: Old Trades Hall, Valette Street, E9 6NU
description: Bedroom-pop/indie songwriter Devon Again plays an intimate Thursday show at MOTH Club. Tickets on DICE.

title: Breathwork, Harmonizing Sound and Sacred Cacao Ceremony
description: Three-hour ceremony combining guided breathwork, a gong and crystal-bowl sound journey, and sacred cacao in a London Fields studio.

title: Miss Grit + mui zyu at MOTH Club
address: Old Trades Hall, Valette Street, E9 6NU
description: Art-rock songwriter Miss Grit double-billed with experimental indie-pop artist mui zyu — a strong small-venue Friday indie bill in Hackney. Tickets on DICE.

title: Forage and Meditate on London Canals (August edition)
description: Guided mindful foraging walk with the Barefoot Forager along the canal towards Hackney Marshes — late-summer wild plants and mushrooms, seasonal tastings, closing meditation.

title: Council Data Hack Day (Campaign Lab)
description: Campaign Lab hackathon building prototypes on a new UK council data scraper — transparency tools, AI-powered search and dashboards for local democracy; coders, designers, researchers and idea-people all welcome.

title: Jahnavi Harrison — An Evening of Kirtan and Song
description: GRAMMY-nominated devotional artist Jahnavi Harrison leads an evening of soulful kirtan and mantra song in the candlelit Union Chapel; optional pre-show supper from 6pm.
